claim: Forward-deployed engineers embedded with policy/operations teams can move from idea to implementation in weeks vs. months/years; one embedded engineer replaced a 1.5M pound consulting contract
actionable: For large organizations with distributed expertise, embed small technical teams directly with operational units; measure impact in time-to-delivery and cost savings vs. traditional consulting
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Market-rate compensation combined with unusually high autonomy and clear mandate can attract exceptional talent (YC founders, PhD researchers, big tech engineers) to work on important non-profit problems
actionable: If recruiting for mission-driven roles, emphasize impact potential and autonomy over salary alone; target people who care about outcome quality (researchers, entrepreneurs) not just compensation
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Extremely high-bar hiring (0.7% acceptance rate) with outside-only recruitment (not promoted from internal) creates higher-performing teams than mixing in internal candidates
actionable: When building high-impact teams, implement brutal hiring standards; outsource recruitment to avoid institutional bias; expect rejection rates above 99%
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Policy simulation tools reduce time to understand impacts of policy decisions and allow teams to test multiple scenarios; AI makes modeling accessible to non-technical policy teams
actionable: For policy organizations, build model-accessible interfaces (natural language input, visual output) that policy experts can use without data science background; focus on explainability
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Delivery red-teaming tools can flag organizational bias in reporting (optimism bias, risk inflation) by comparing historical accuracy of teams to current projections
actionable: Build historical accuracy baselines for teams/departments; when new projections arrive, compare against baseline bias; surface systematic over/under-estimation to decision makers
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Public-facing dashboards showing government project delivery status improve transparency and accountability; lack of such dashboards indicates untapped public data opportunities
actionable: For government/public organizations, prioritize public dashboards of key metrics; use AI to automatically generate and update dashboards from existing reporting systems
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: LLM-based legal document analysis (Extract tool) can digitize handwritten planning documents, unblocking bottlenecks; Extract deployed across England's local authorities
actionable: For document-intensive processes, audit manual handwritten/scanned document workflows; use OCR + LLMs to digitize and extract structured data; measure cycle-time reduction
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: AI tutoring must be evaluated for safety and cognitive load impact before classroom rollout; benchmarking frontier models against pedagogical metrics, not just benchmark scores
actionable: Before deploying LLM-based tutors in schools, test against cognitive load metrics and learning outcomes (not just accuracy); involve educators in evaluation, not just researchers
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Forward-deployed engineers in prisons working with parole officers and wardens can identify AI use cases for drug interdiction and process automation that HQ-based teams miss
actionable: For specialized/domain-specific problems, embed AI engineers with frontline staff, not just management; frontline workers know their pain points better than executives
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)

claim: Small elite teams at the center can create outsized impact when given political backing, autonomy, and market-rate pay; insurgent model can scale through spin-out teams
actionable: If building transformation team in large organization, focus on impact per person not headcount; enable team members to spin off and build new teams rather than centralizing everything
source_title: Rewiring the State — Eoin Mulgrew, No. 10 (Downing Street)
